sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Hypogonadism
History of Present Illness
The patient reports being a 59-year-old with a history of hypogonadism. He was last seen on November 24, 2020. He is currently taking 1 mg of testosterone every 10 days, and finasteride. He also takes cialis daily. He reports feeling more vigorous and sleeping well since starting testosterone, and notes improvements in his bloodwork. He prefers paper prescriptions. He has been injecting testosterone for approximately eight days. He is considering increasing his dose to 175 mg.
Findings
The patient's cholesterol and triglycerides have decreased significantly, from 265 mg/dL to 145 mg/dL. His red blood cell count has increased. Physical exam reveals normal heart and lung sounds. PSA is 0.6.
Assessment
The patient presents with hypogonadism, evidenced by decreased PSA and testosterone levels. His cholesterol and triglycerides have shown significant improvement with testosterone replacement therapy.
Plan
The doctor will get the prescriptions. The patient prefers paper prescriptions.
